no acute cardiopulmonary abnormality.base_explanation: This means no urgent problems involving the heart or lungs were seen on the scan at the time of imaging.
no acute cardiopulmonary abnormality.follow_up_note: This is generally a reassuring finding.

mild cardiomegaly.base_explanation: This term indicates a slightly enlarged heart size seen on imaging. On its own, it does not confirm heart disease.
mild cardiomegaly.follow_up_note: Doctors may correlate this finding with symptoms or other tests if needed.

moderate cardiomegaly.base_explanation: This finding suggests a more noticeable increase in heart size compared to mild cardiomegaly.
moderate cardiomegaly.follow_up_note: Clinical evaluation helps determine the significance of this finding.

pulmonary edema.base_explanation: Pulmonary edema refers to fluid accumulation in the lungs, which can affect breathing.
pulmonary edema.follow_up_note: This finding usually requires prompt medical evaluation.

pleural effusion.base_explanation: A pleural effusion means there is fluid present in the space surrounding the lungs.
pleural effusion.follow_up_note: The cause and significance depend on the amount of fluid and clinical context.

pericardial effusion.base_explanation: This term refers to fluid accumulation in the sac surrounding the heart.
pericardial effusion.follow_up_note: Further evaluation helps determine whether this finding is clinically important.

atelectasis.base_explanation: Atelectasis means a small portion of the lung is not fully expanded. This can occur due to shallow breathing or mucus blockage.
atelectasis.follow_up_note: This finding is often temporary and resolves with appropriate care.

lung consolidation.base_explanation: Lung consolidation refers to an area of the lung that appears denser than normal, often due to fluid, infection, or inflammation.
lung consolidation.follow_up_note: Doctors correlate this with symptoms and laboratory findings.

pulmonary nodule.base_explanation: A pulmonary nodule is a small, rounded spot seen in the lung. Many nodules are benign, especially when small.
pulmonary nodule.follow_up_note: Size, appearance, and risk factors guide further evaluation.

pneumothorax.base_explanation: A pneumothorax means there is air in the space around the lung, which can cause part of the lung to collapse.
pneumothorax.follow_up_note: This finding typically requires urgent medical assessment.
